title: Earnings: As of December 31, 2026
content:
CAMPUS reported net income of $1.7 million as of December 31, 2026. Earnings continue to be supported primarily by net interest income, reflecting the credit union's sizeable investment portfolio and controlled funding costs. Interest income totaled $10.8 million, while interest expense amounted to $3.0 million, generating steady spread income that remains the primary contributor to profitability.

Non-interest income totaled $261K and remains a modest but stable contributor to overall revenue. The majority of this income is generated from fee income ($19) and other non-interest income ($138), with additional contributions from credit loss expense ($4). These revenue streams provide supplemental diversification beyond interest income, though the overall contribution remains relatively small compared with core lending and investment earnings.

Non-interest expense totaled $8.6 million, reflecting the ongoing costs required to support daily operations and member services. The largest component is employee compensation (not explicitly broken out in source), followed by office occupancy expense ($972K) and professional and outside services ($41). Other operating expenses—including miscellaneous operating expenses, travel, education, insurance, and loan servicing expenses—remain relatively small and consistent with the credit union's scale. Overall expense levels appear stable and aligned with operational needs.

Overall Position:
CAMPUS continues to generate stable earnings supported by net interest income and controlled operating expenses. While non-interest income remains limited and expenses reflect necessary operational costs, the institution maintains positive profitability. Sustaining earnings performance will depend on preserving spread income, maintaining expense discipline, and gradually expanding diversified revenue sources to support long-term financial stability.
